title: Cysteine as a Selective Reagent for Spectrophotometric Determination of Osmium(VIII)
author: C. L. SHARMA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, University of Roorkee, Roorkee-241 667 

  Manuscript received 9 July 1984, revised 11 January 1985, accepted 27 April 1985  

 Cysteine forms a 1 :1 coloured complex with Os VIII  in aqueous solution. The yellowish brown complex exhibits a maximum absorbance at 370 nm in the  p H range 4.0-5.2. Beer's law is obeyed from 1.0-60.0 ppm, the optimum range being 5.0-55.0 ppm. The Sandell's sensitivity of the reaction is 0.132  µ g cm -2  and the molar absorptivity of the complex is 1429 dm 3  mol -1  cm -1 .
